content: TBI management depends on severity. Mild TBI/concussion: cognitive and physical rest 24-48 hours, then gradual return to activity; avoid second impact before symptom resolution (second impact syndrome—rare but catastrophic). Analgesics for headache (avoid opioids initially), antiemetics. Moderate-severe TBI: ABCs, cervical spine protection, avoid hypotension (SBP <90) and hypoxia (SpO2 <90%) which worsen outcomes. ICP management (target ICP <22 mmHg): head elevation 30°, sedation (propofol), osmotic therapy (mannitol 0.25-1g/kg or hypertonic saline 3-23.4%), CSF drainage via EVD, neuromuscular blockade. Decompressive craniectomy for refractory elevated ICP (RESCUEicp trial showed survival benefit but more vegetative state survivors). Seizure prophylaxis: levetiracetam or phenytoin × 7 days only (prevents early seizures, not late). Rehabilitation: early mobilization, PT/OT/speech therapy, cognitive rehabilitation. Chronic issues: spasticity (baclofen, botulinum toxin), post-traumatic headache, mood disorders, cognitive impairment, post-traumatic hypopituitarism (screen for GH, gonadotropin deficiency).
